sourceMovie.title: Trap
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Trap (2024) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Split
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Split (2017) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps Trap rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Silence of the Lambs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Silence of the Lambs (1991) is the kind of film Trap fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The crime overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Natural Born Killers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Natural Born Killers shares Trap's gift for crime — the 1994 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: A History of Violence
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Trap compelling — the crime, the thriller — shows up in A History of Violence (2005) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Number 23
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of Trap is alive in The Number 23 (2007), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Call
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Call (2013) operates in the same crime as Trap, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Unhinged
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Unhinged (2020) belongs to the same conversation as Trap — both anchor their horror in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Frequency
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trap's crime stayed with you, Frequency (2000) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Voices
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Trap excels in crime, The Voices (2014) doubles down on horror to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Mr. Brooks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mr. Brooks (2007) is the natural next step after Trap: same crime DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after Trap?
faq.answer: Try Split, The Silence of the Lambs, Natural Born Killers—each shares DNA with Trap while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Trap?
faq.answer: Trap (2024) is best described as Crime, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Trap worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
